### Post by tad1073 on 2011-02-06
I just got a new LCD TV and the resolution is 1920x1080. I have it connected to my computer from YPbPr component out on the tv to s-video on the video card. The highest resolution I have acheived is 1024x768, I need some help configuring xorg so I can acheive the native resolution of the tv. I have tried some guides and tutorials but had no luck.

current xorg.conf
```

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 260.19.36  (buildmeister@swio-display-x86-rhel47-01.nvidia.com)  Tue Jan 18 17:15:22 PST 2011

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 260.19.06  (buildd@yellow)  Mon Oct  4 15:59:51 UTC 2010

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "LG electronics"
    ModelName      "TV-0"
    VendorName     "LG electronics"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 55.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 120.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 9500 GT"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "1"
    Option         "metamodes" "TV: nvidia-auto-select +1680+0, DFP: nvidia-auto-select +0+0"
    Option         "Coolbits" "1"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection


```

### Post by Rocket2DMn on 2011-02-06
Please wait 24 hours before bumping your thread to give the community members a chance to respond.

I typically don't see S-video give very high resolutions, 1024x768 may be the limit you can get.  What video card model are you using?  If it has a digital out (like DVI), you can get a simple adapter (or separate cable) to convert the DVI to HDMI, which your TV probably supports.

Also, using the NVidia configuration utility (System->Administration->NVIDIA X Server Settings if you're using restricted drivers) may be easier than trying to get this configured in xorg.conf - we rarely work with this file manually anymore.

---

### Post by tad1073 on 2011-02-06
I have a Nvidia 9500 GT w/2 DVI-D ports and an s-video port. With nvidia-settings 1024x768 is the highest resolution available through s-video, that is why I was trying to configure xorg. When in Windows 7 I am able to acheive 1920x1080 using the s-video. I think my best bet is going to be getting a DVI-D to HDMI cable, I found one at Walmart for $9+tax. I don't know how good it will be considering how cheap it is.

And sorry for bumping after only 5 hours but I am kinda anxious to get it working.
